bead.title: ddx work: each attempt runs in an isolated worktree; failure reverts/cleans before next attempt
bead.description: Decomposed from ddx-2a24727c (wedged: too large). SCOPE: every execute-bead attempt (incl. escalation retry) runs in an isolated git worktree; on failure the worktree is reverted/cleaned so the canonical repo stays clean and the next attempt starts from a known base. NON-SCOPE: the dirty-root guard (sibling child). Self-contained.
bead.acceptance: 1. New scoped test (internal/agent, bounded): a failed attempt leaves the canonical repo clean; a retry starts from a clean isolated worktree. 2. cd /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run IsolatedWorktree -count=1 -timeout 120s green. 3. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: Worktree isolation is already implemented in WorktreeAttemptBackend (attempt_backend.go:97-142) with deferred Cleanup() on failure and git isolation wrappers in execute_bead_git_isolation.go. The bead's AC is test-only: add TestIsolatedWorktree verifying canonical repo cleanliness after failure and clean-start for retry. Existing tests cover partial-add failure (TestExecuteBeadWorktreeAddFailure_RemovesPartialDir) and lost-worktree diagnostics, but no lifecycle test of the form 'failed attempt → clean canonical → retry starts isolated'. An agent reading the AC first will correctly target test authorship. Minor ambiguity: description prose reads as if implementation is needed, but AC is unambiguously test-only.","fingerprint":"73221f55824032df5378ad5ffe4c5e79d908cc4e23e38e20585acbd79318cf52","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"73221f55824032df5378ad5ffe4c5e79d908cc4e23e38e20585acbd79318cf52"}
